intro: Moving from JavaScript to Dart changes an application foundation from a dynamic web architecture to a multi-platform compilation framework, frequently used with Flutter. This migration enables strong compile-time type checks, predictable performance targets across platforms, and direct native compilation options. Developers transition to gain a unified programming model that delivers highly optimized web, mobile, and desktop binary executions.

diffs.from: Dynamic types where objects change properties fluidly at runtime with weak safety.
diffs.to: Statically typed system ensuring type safety while utilizing inferencing with optional sound dynamic escape hatches.

diffs.from: Prototypal object syntax supporting flexible function boundaries and variable keyword variants.
diffs.to: Class-based object-oriented structure matching modern C-style language rules with explicit visibility interfaces.

diffs.from: Managed via browser or Node engines using generational collection sweeps on the heap.
diffs.to: Managed via a fast generational scavenger garbage collector specialized for short-lived UI widget life cycles.

diffs.from: Single-thread event loop managing asynchronous queues with macro-tasks and Promises.
diffs.to: Isolate-based concurrency architecture utilizing Futures, Streams, and dedicated memory spaces per thread.

diffs.from: Basic array manipulation APIs, regular expressions, and minimal OS interfaces.
diffs.to: Rich collection packages, cryptographic bindings, core file/network IO utilities, and native platform channels.

examples.description: Declaring a class containing basic fields and initialization steps.

examples.title: Asynchronous Streams
examples.description: Defining a generator context to produce values over time.

tips.title: Adhere to Sound Null Safety
tips.body: Dart separates types into non-nullable and nullable categories. If a variable can contain a null state, you must explicitly mark its type descriptor with a question mark suffix (e.g., `String?`), otherwise compiler diagnostics prevent build tasks.

tips.title: Transition from Prototypes to Classes
tips.body: JavaScript allows updating functional prototypes to add instance features globally. In Dart, all architectural structures use concrete classes, mixins, or extension methods to patch runtime type behavior cleanly.

tips.title: Understand Isolates Over Web Workers
tips.body: Dart isolates share no memory boundaries with one another. Unlike JavaScript background workers which communicate using structured serialization, data passing between isolates utilizes specialized hardware port channels.

tips.title: Use Named Constructors
tips.body: JavaScript only permits one constructor method per class definition. Dart solves this restriction by introducing named constructors (e.g., `Point.fromJson(Map data)`), which increases instantiation context transparency.
